project_topic: An integrated methodology for measuring universal and meaningful connectivity (UMC) in highly connected cities
scope: Section 3.3 platform-visible signal analysis, Bayesian aggregation, and agent-based interpretation

repository_boundaries.privacy_rule: Do not expose raw platform text, post IDs, writer fields, local credentials, or full post-level LLM dumps in public/report artifacts.

sections.purpose: Construct Seoul district-level UMC scores for 2023 and 2024 and produce report-ready spatial/statistical figures.

transformations: Deduplicate physical station coordinates
Spatially join base stations to Seoul district boundaries
Compute 4G and 5G station-density indicators
validation_checks: Prints deduplication coverage and district-mapping counts
Reports density ranges

transformations: Read monthly living-population files
Use representative dates and daytime hours
Aggregate to district-year daytime living-population denominators
validation_checks: Reports Seoul district count
Reports minimum/maximum daytime living-population values

transformations: Auto-detect monthly telecom workbooks
Aggregate neighborhood-level telecom indicators to district level with population weights
Compute annual mobile data usage, online service days, and fee delinquency rate
validation_checks: Reports yearly ranges for delinquency, mobile data, and online service days
Reports year-to-year change summaries

transformations: Compute infrastructure/connectivity from station density and download speed
Compute availability from mobile data use, online service days, and Wi-Fi density
Compute affordability from income and telecom fee delinquency with burden reversal
Compute devices, digital skills, and safety from weighted digital-competency survey items
Apply min-max normalization and same-direction scoring
Create dimension scores, composite UMC score, and district ranks
validation_checks: Reports top districts by UMC score
Reports dimension means
Reports rank and score changes across years

transformations: Generate heatmaps, rankings, profile charts, maps, and LISA/Moran diagnostics
validation_checks: generate_section31_figures.py enforces 25 districts
Moran and LISA statistics are exported for report verification

report_outputs: Part 1 UMC score inputs for Sections 3.2 and 3.3

sections.purpose: Estimate associations between individual characteristics, district-level UMC conditions, and individual digital service use among Seoul Survey respondents.

inputs: Part 1 district-year L2 files: umc_l2_variables_2023.csv and umc_l2_variables_2024.csv
transformations: Pool Seoul Survey 2023 and 2024 respondent files
Map district codes and harmonize changed survey fields
Construct digital_use_score from eight digital service-use items scaled to 0-80
Create binary robustness outcomes from selected Q3 items
Recode demographic, socioeconomic, household, housing, and vulnerability covariates
Grand-mean center age and income
Merge time-varying Part 1 UMC district-year predictors
Normalize main Level 2 predictors and raw indicators
Drop unresolved missing dependent-variable and required-control rows
validation_checks: Reports respondent counts by year
Checks L2 merge coverage
Runs VIF scan for Level 1 predictors
Screens Level 2 correlations

transformations: Create Level 1 and Level 2 descriptive statistics
Create vulnerable-group comparisons and exploratory figures
validation_checks: Descriptive table dimensions and variable names checked against report claims

model_sequence: Model 0: null random-intercept model by district_code
Model 2: Level 1 plus district-level UMC predictors
Model 3: cross-level interaction model for elderly and low-education vulnerability
validation_checks: ICC and design effect from Model 0
Likelihood-ratio tests across nested models
Fallback estimation when Level 2 variance collapses
Residual checks for random effects and Level 1 residuals
Sensitivity tests: year split, endogenous expansion, low-education district exclusion, raw indicator replacement
Policy simulation confidence intervals and interpretation boundaries
validity_check_report.md records exogeneity, causal-limit, parameter-count, robustness, and interpretation checks

interpretation_boundary: HLM is association/decomposition analysis, not causal identification.
District-level effects must be interpreted cautiously because Level 2 has 25 Seoul districts.

sections.section_name: Platform-Visible Signal Analysis, Bayesian Aggregation, and Agent-Based Interpretation
sections.purpose: Identify platform-visible lived-experience signals of UMC-related deprivation and integrate them with measured district UMC priors without treating platform posts as a representative population sample.

transformations: Clean raw post records and remove unusable text
Filter candidate posts with UMC keyword dictionaries
Split candidates by district
Generate markdown batches for LLM classification
Parse LLM table responses
Merge parsed labels back to candidate metadata

classification_agent.input_contract: Markdown table with ID and text
classification_agent.output_contract: Four columns: ID, UMC Related, UMC Dimension, Problem Summary
decision_rules: Y only when digital connectivity is the primary concern
N when digital is absent, peripheral, or merely a tool/background
Question mark for ambiguous digital relevance
Dimension names must use official English UMC dimension names
overclassification_controls: Restaurant/cafe, offline skill learning, lost items, housing, vehicle, health, and general life posts remain N unless the digital-connectivity problem is primary
Danggeun transaction disputes are not Safety & Security unless they are online/cybersecurity threats

human_validation_layer.privacy_note: Reviewer files include internal join keys and short text excerpts for checking; do not reproduce row-level content in public outputs.

transformations: Restore only safe metadata: gu, regionName, createdAt
Map gu x regionName to administrative-dong candidates
Use equal allocation_weight for ambiguous region-to-dong candidates
Join posts to living population at date-hour-living_pop_dong_code exact grain
Exclude official living-population exact cells that are absent without interpolation
validation_checks: Parsed files: 3004
Classified rows: 131792
Dong source match rate: 100 percent
Allocated post weight total: 131792.0 before living-population exclusions
Exact matched density rows: 145826
Two missing official living-population cells excluded

transformations: Create dimension flags only when umc_related == Y and a dimension pattern is present
Aggregate Y posts and authoring-time living-population exposure by district and UMC dimension
Use Part 1 district-dimension deficiency scores as administrative prior information
Estimate moment-based kappa separately by dimension rather than using a fixed pseudo-count
Compute precision-weighted prior and observed posting rates per 100,000 person-years
Report shift_per100k and within-dimension standardized z_shift
validation_checks: eb_shrinkage_posterior.csv has 450 rows: 3 scenarios x 25 districts x 6 dimensions
eb_shrinkage_summary.csv has 150 rows
table5_eb_prior_posterior.csv has 25 district rows
147 of 150 district-dimension cells are sign-stable across mapping specifications
total_y_posts = 7136 and citywide_rate_per100k = 46.4437 in eb_shrinkage_report_notes.json
interpretation_boundary: Bayesian updating is exploratory evidence integration, not prevalence estimation.
Positive and negative posterior shifts are directional platform-prior divergences, not causal claims.

agents.name: abductive-reasoner
agents.information_set: post text, basic region/time metadata, dim_codes, and shared coding scheme only

agents.name: forward-reasoner
agents.information_set: post text, basic region/time metadata, dim_codes, and shared coding scheme only

agents.name: sequential-reasoner
agents.information_set: post text, basic region/time metadata, dim_codes, and shared coding scheme only

agents.name: data-spec
agents.role: Stage C variable-request specification from three hypotheses and variable_catalog
agents.information_set: three region_hypotheses, region fields, and config/data_sources.json variable_catalog

agents.name: judgment-synthesizer
agents.role: Stage E evidence-grounded verdict and final coding
agents.information_set: three hypotheses, data_spec verification rules, evidence.json values, and coding_scheme

validation_checks: Stage B reasoners are isolated from each other and from regional aggregate data
Stage B outputs contain falsifiable region_hypotheses only, with no confidence/layer/classification
Stage C requests only variables registered in config/data_sources.json
Stage D builds evidence deterministically and records unavailable/not_found values instead of inventing data
Stage E assigns supported only when evidence directly satisfies the verification rule
absence_typology.json A1-F1 coding is retired from final classification

stage_b_target_set.status: Stage B complete for the current target set

stage_c_d_target_set.status: Stage C data specs and Stage D evidence exist for the current target set

stage_e_judgments.status: Stage E is still being accumulated and is not used as a substantive report result
stage_e_judgments.count_note: Count was still changing during report review; do not use as a substantive distribution.

district_hypothesis_groups.privacy: aggregate table; excludes post IDs, grounded spans, and claim text

interpretation_boundary: Platform posts are platform-visible lived-experience signals, not representative prevalence data.
LLMs are structured coding assistants and judgment support tools, not autonomous evidence sources.
Post-level inference examples must remain anonymized and paraphrased in report-facing text.

global_validation_checks: Root report repo and nested analysis repos must be checked separately before commits.
No raw data, private text, post IDs, or local credentials may be staged.
Report claims must cite source tables, figures, scripts, validation notes, or rendered DOCX pages.
Section 3.1 outputs feed Section 3.2 Level 2 predictors and Section 3.3 Bayesian priors.
Section 3.3 Bayesian results must remain framed as exploratory platform-prior divergence.

subagent_evidence_used.task: Extract PPT data-source information from ppt/High-Five_20260507.pptx
Map Section 3.1 Part 1 UMC index pipeline
